fault_hypothesis: Line 82 uses simple `int()` (floor/truncation) instead of the largest-remainder (Hamilton) method, so when allocations are non-integer the remainders are discarded and the sum of results is less than `total`.

spec.expr: sum([int(v) for v in shares__1]) == int(total)

constraints.intent: The integer allocations produced by truncation must sum exactly to total; this is FALSE when remainders are discarded.
Raw shares must sum to total before integer rounding is applied.
After the floor_to_int branch, the returned integer list must sum to total.
Shares list length must match weights length before being consumed.
